prompt: Explain the attention mechanism in transformer models to a software engineer who has never done ML. Keep it to about 200 words.
response:
Attention is a learned routing mechanism that lets each token in a sequence “look at” other tokens and build a context-aware representation.

How it works (high level):
- For every token you compute three vectors by learned linear projections: Query (Q), Key (K), and Value (V). Think of Q as a request, K as an address, and V as the payload.
- For a given token, compute dot products between its Q and all tokens’ K to get similarity scores. Scale and apply softmax to turn scores into attention weights (a probability distribution).
- The token’s new representation is the weighted sum of all V vectors using those weights. So it aggregates information from other tokens proportionally to relevance.

Key points for a software engineer:
- Self-attention: Q, K, V all come from the same sequence, so every token can attend to every other token (global context).
- Multi-head attention runs several independent Q/K/V projections in parallel so the model can capture different relation types, then concatenates results.
- Implementationally this is matrix-heavy (lots of linear algebra), highly parallelizable on GPUs, and has O(n^2) time/memory in sequence length.
- Intuition: a differentiable, learned pointer system that routes and mixes information between positions.
